purpose: Canonical structure assignment for a material explorer. A material variant inherits a base polymer structure and receives a separate additive/formulation badge. It must never receive a fabricated new repeat unit merely because it contains CF, GF, ESD, FR, colour, glow pigment, recycled feedstock, or a marketing-grade label.

rendering_contract.required_visual: Use inline SVG chemical diagrams designed and stored locally in the project. Do not scrape Google Images, hotlink image files, use watermarked stock art, or use a generic ball-and-stick picture.
rendering_contract.color_rule: Use element labels and bonds as the primary information. Optional restrained colors are supplementary only; never rely on red/grey atoms with no labels.
rendering_contract.safety_rule: For exact standard polymers, show a repeat unit. For blends/copolymers/graft polymers or proprietary umbrella grades, show an explicitly labelled schematic rather than an invented exact repeat unit.
rendering_contract.composite_rule: CF/GF/metal/mineral/wood/etc. are additives or reinforcements. Draw the base resin structure plus a clearly separate modifier badge/overlay; do not put a fibre or filler into the polymer repeat unit unless an actual chemical copolymer is known.
rendering_contract.accessibility_rule: Each SVG needs a title, desc, aria-label, semantic visible caption, and high-contrast dark/light mode support.

pla.title: Poly(lactic acid) / Polylactide
pla.diagram_note: Show the ester backbone, the CH₃ side group, bracket and n. PLA stereochemistry (PLLA/PDLA/PDLLA) can differ and is intentionally not implied by this default drawing.

pet.title: Poly(ethylene terephthalate)
pet.diagram_note: Draw a para-phenylene ring between the two carbonyl groups. Do not draw PETG with this as an exact composition.

petg_copolyester.title: PETG / glycol-modified PET copolyester
petg_copolyester.formula: PET-like terephthalate backbone + variable glycol comonomer(s)
petg_copolyester.diagram_note: Render the PET terephthalate section as the primary motif and a clearly labelled dashed “modified glycol comonomer” segment. Do not present one exact repeat unit as universal PETG chemistry.

copolyester_generic.title: Engineering copolyester
copolyester_generic.chemistry_status: not a single standardized repeat unit
copolyester_generic.diagram_note: Use a clean polyester-chain schematic with labelled variable comonomer block. Never invent a single molecular structure for CPE, CPE+, CoPET, PCTG or similar commercial umbrella terms.

abs_graft.title: Acrylonitrile–butadiene–styrene
abs_graft.formula: polybutadiene rubber backbone/domain with poly(styrene-co-acrylonitrile) grafts
abs_graft.chemistry_status: terpolymer/graft system; ratio and morphology vary
abs_graft.diagram_note: Do not show ABS as one false homopolymer repeat unit. Show a rubbery butadiene backbone/domain and labelled SAN graft chains. Include “composition varies by grade”.

asa_graft.title: Acrylonitrile–styrene–acrylate
asa_graft.chemistry_status: terpolymer/graft system; ratio and morphology vary
asa_graft.diagram_note: Use an acrylic-rubber core/domain with labelled SAN graft chains. Make clear that ASA is not a single exact repeat unit.

polystyrene.title: Polystyrene
polystyrene.diagram_note: Show phenyl side group attached to the vinyl backbone.

hips.title: High-impact polystyrene
hips.diagram_note: Show a PS chain plus distinct rubber phase/graft motif; do not label the result as a pure PS repeat unit.

san_random.title: Styrene–acrylonitrile copolymer
san_random.diagram_note: Use a random/alternating-looking chain with two labelled repeat motifs and x/y composition notation.

tpu_segmented.title: Thermoplastic polyurethane
tpu_segmented.diagram_note: Use a clean repeated urethane linkage and visually separate the “soft segment” R from “hard segment” R′. Shore hardness is not a different molecular structure.

tpe_generic.title: Thermoplastic elastomer
tpe_generic.formula: Hard thermoplastic domains + soft elastomer domains
tpe_generic.diagram_note: Render a two-domain elastomer schematic. Do not show one exact chain because TPE is a product-class umbrella.

tpc_segmented.title: Thermoplastic copolyester elastomer
tpc_segmented.diagram_note: Show alternating hard polyester and soft segment blocks, labelled as representative rather than exact.

tpv_blend.title: Thermoplastic vulcanizate
tpv_blend.formula: thermoplastic matrix + dynamically vulcanized elastomer network
tpv_blend.chemistry_status: multiphase material, not one repeat unit
tpv_blend.diagram_note: Use a thermoplastic chain with embedded crosslinked elastomer-domain illustration. Avoid a fake single polymer repeat unit.

pa_generic.title: Polyamide / nylon
pa_generic.diagram_note: Show amide linkage and a labelled variable R spacer. Use only where the nylon grade is not identified.

pa6.title: Polyamide 6
pa6.diagram_note: Show the amide and five-methylene spacer.

pa11.title: Polyamide 11
pa11.diagram_note: Show the amide and ten-methylene spacer.

pa12.title: Polyamide 12
pa12.diagram_note: Show the amide and eleven-methylene spacer.

pa66.title: Polyamide 66
pa66.diagram_note: Show both amide groups, hexamethylene-diamine and adipate spacer.

pa610.title: Polyamide 610
pa610.diagram_note: Show both amide groups, hexamethylene diamine and sebacate spacer.

pa612.title: Polyamide 612
pa612.diagram_note: Show both amide groups, hexamethylene diamine and dodecanedioate spacer.

pa_copolymer.title: Polyamide copolymer
pa_copolymer.diagram_note: Show two amide repeat motifs with labelled variable spacer blocks. Do not call it a precise PA6/PA12 structure unless specified.

paht.title: High-temperature polyamide
paht.formula: High-temperature aliphatic/semi-aromatic polyamide; exact chemistry is grade-specific
paht.chemistry_status: commercial family; not one universal repeat unit
paht.diagram_note: Use a representative amide linkage with a “high-temperature nylon grade” label. Do not draw PAHT-CF as a chemically different backbone.

ppa.title: Polyphthalamide
ppa.formula: semi-aromatic polyamide with phthalate ring(s) + aliphatic diamine segment
ppa.diagram_note: Draw an amide linkage containing an aromatic phthalate ring and a clearly labelled variable aliphatic spacer. Mark “representative PPA repeat; exact grade varies”.

pc.title: Bisphenol-A polycarbonate
pc.diagram_note: Draw both phenyl rings, central C(CH₃)₂ bridge and carbonate linkage.

pc_blend_generic.title: Polycarbonate blend
pc_blend_generic.diagram_note: Show the PC repeat plus a dashed “second polymer varies by product” component. Do not guess the blend partner.

pc_abs_blend.title: PC–ABS blend
pc_abs_blend.diagram_note: Display two linked mini-diagrams: PC repeat and ABS graft schematic; label it a blend, not a copolymer.

pc_pbt_blend.title: PC–PBT blend
pc_pbt_blend.diagram_note: Display PC and PBT mini-diagrams separately; do not merge them into a fictional repeat unit.

pc_pet_blend.title: PC–PET blend
pc_pet_blend.diagram_note: Display PC and PET mini-diagrams separately; label exact proportions as product-dependent.

pc_ptfe_blend.title: PC–PTFE blend
pc_ptfe_blend.diagram_note: Display PC repeat plus a PTFE chain [–CF₂–CF₂–]ₙ; never imply that PTFE is chemically attached unless the product TDS says so.

pbt.title: Poly(butylene terephthalate)
pbt.diagram_note: Use a four-methylene glycol section and para-phenylene terephthalate section.

pmma.title: Poly(methyl methacrylate)
pmma.diagram_note: Show vinyl backbone, CH₃ group and methyl ester side group.

pom.title: Polyoxymethylene
pom.diagram_note: For copolymer POM grades, use the same base motif with a note that small comonomer amounts may be present.

pp.title: Polypropylene
pp.diagram_note: Show methyl side group; do not claim isotactic/syndiotactic/atactic tacticity unless product data exists.

pe.title: Polyethylene
pe.diagram_note: Use the same repeat for HDPE and LDPE; their difference is architecture/branching, which should appear as a small note/badge, not a different monomer.

eva.title: Ethylene–vinyl acetate copolymer
eva.chemistry_status: copolymer composition varies by VA content
eva.diagram_note: Show ethylene and vinyl acetate units with x/y notation.

pvb.title: Poly(vinyl butyral)
pvb.formula: poly(vinyl alcohol) backbone with butyral acetal groups and residual OH/acetate groups
pvb.chemistry_status: partial acetal; composition varies by residual hydroxyl/acetate level
pvb.diagram_note: Use a labelled PVA-derived backbone and a butyral-acetal side/bridge motif. Mark as representative, not a universal single repeat unit.

sbs.title: Styrene–butadiene–styrene
sbs.chemistry_status: block copolymer; block lengths vary by grade
sbs.diagram_note: Show three labelled blocks, not one random repeat: polystyrene / polybutadiene / polystyrene.

sebs.title: Styrene–ethylene/butylene–styrene
sebs.chemistry_status: hydrogenated SBS block copolymer; composition varies
sebs.diagram_note: Show three labelled blocks and note that the middle block is hydrogenated butadiene-derived material.

pvdf.title: Poly(vinylidene fluoride)
pvdf.diagram_note: Show CH₂ and CF₂ backbone; do not imply a specific crystalline phase.

pps.title: Polyphenylene sulfide
pps.diagram_note: Draw a para-phenylene ring linked by sulfur atoms.

peek.title: Polyether ether ketone
peek.diagram_note: Draw three para-phenylene rings connected by ether, ether, and ketone linkages.

pekk.title: Polyether ketone ketone
pekk.chemistry_status: repeat-regioisomer distribution can vary by grade
pekk.diagram_note: Use the aryl ether ketone ketone motif and add a small note that tere-/isophthalic arrangement varies by grade.

pei.title: Polyetherimide
pei.formula: aromatic ether-imide repeating motif (common BPA dianhydride / aromatic diamine architecture)
pei.chemistry_status: common commercial PEI architecture; product grade can vary
pei.diagram_note: Render a clearly labelled aromatic ether-imide motif. It must contain an imide ring/functionality, not merely “aromatic polymer”.

psu.title: Polysulfone
psu.formula: aromatic ether–sulfone backbone (common bisphenol-A polysulfone motif)
psu.diagram_note: Draw an aromatic backbone with –SO₂– and ether linkages; use a note when a grade is not BPA-polysulfone.

ppsu.title: Polyphenylsulfone
ppsu.formula: aromatic ether–sulfone backbone with additional phenyl content
ppsu.diagram_note: Render an aromatic ether-sulfone motif; mark as representative rather than a universal repeat drawing.

pesu.title: Polyethersulfone
pesu.diagram_note: Render aromatic rings joined through ether and sulfone groups, labelled “representative PESU repeat”.

lcp.title: Liquid-crystal polymer
lcp.formula: rigid aromatic copolyester / copolyamide sequence; exact monomers vary
lcp.chemistry_status: umbrella family; no single universal repeat unit
lcp.diagram_note: Use a rigid-rod aromatic chain schematic with “composition varies by grade”. Do not claim every LCP uses the same monomers.

pva.title: Poly(vinyl alcohol)
pva.chemistry_status: standard repeating unit; degree of hydrolysis varies
pva.diagram_note: Show hydroxyl side group. Add a small note that residual acetate content and hydrolysis level affect real PVA grades.

bvoh.title: Butenediol vinyl alcohol copolymer
bvoh.formula: vinyl alcohol copolymer with proprietary comonomer content
bvoh.chemistry_status: commercial copolymer; composition varies by manufacturer
bvoh.diagram_note: Use PVA-like vinyl alcohol motif plus a dashed labelled comonomer block. Do not fabricate a precise universal BVOH structure.

soft_pla.title: Soft / flexible PLA blend
soft_pla.chemistry_status: product-defined blend, not one standardized polymer
soft_pla.diagram_note: Display the PLA repeat plus a flexible-modifier badge/secondary chain. Do not label it as a distinct PLA homopolymer.

support_breakaway.title: Breakaway support material
support_breakaway.diagram_note: Use a neutral support-material graphic with “exact chemistry is manufacturer-specific”. Do not invent a molecular repeat unit.

modifier_only.title: Composite/additive modifier
modifier_only.formula: Must be combined with a base polymer
modifier_only.diagram_note: Show the named filler icon/pattern and an explicit “base resin required” label. Never render a fictional polymer repeat unit.

Support PC.structural_relation: PC-compatible support formulation; exact support chemistry is product-specific

HIPS Support.structural_relation: HIPS material used as a removable support; base chemistry remains HIPS

PP-CF.structural_relation: PP base resin with filler; not a new PP repeat unit

PP-GF.structural_relation: PP base resin with filler; not a new PP repeat unit
